title: Simultaneous Effects
excerpt: When multiple effects would resolve at the same time, the system needs an ordering rule. Simultaneous effects share a trigger moment, but their resolution is still sequenced.
markdown:
# Simultaneous Effects

Simultaneous effects occur when two or more rules, features, hazards, or triggered results would resolve at the same moment. They share a timing window, but their consequences still need an ordered resolution.

## Core Resolution

### Shared Trigger, Ordered Outcome
If multiple effects are created by the same moment, they are treated as simultaneous for timing purposes. Their outcomes are then resolved in a defined order so later results can take earlier changes into account.

### Order Matters When State Changes
Resolution order matters whenever an earlier effect changes position, health, targeting validity, availability of a [[reaction]], or whether a later effect can still apply. Simultaneous timing does not mean all results are applied as one indistinguishable block.

### Use the Governing Rule When It Exists
Some spells, features, or subsystems define their own ordering language. When a governing rule provides an explicit sequence, that sequence overrides any broader default handling.

## Practical Meaning

Simultaneous effects are a timing bucket, not a promise that every result lands in a perfectly merged state. The system still needs a coherent sequence whenever multiple outcomes compete for the same moment of resolution.
